stage.name: Cryptocurrency Guide
stage.description: UTXO model, secp256k1 elliptic-curve cryptography, mining economics, altcoins, and on-chain analysis.

elements.content: UTXO model, supply mechanics, and what makes XRP different

Bitcoin's UTXO Model

Bitcoin has no 'accounts'. It tracks Unspent Transaction Outputs (UTXOs).

To send 1 BTC you consume existing UTXOs and create new ones — like breaking a $20 bill into change.

Privacy benefit: each tx uses fresh outputs. Privacy risk: UTXO graph analysis can trace flows.

XRP vs ETH: Key Numbers

XRP: 3–5 sec finality · $0.0002 fee · 1,500 tx/s
ETH: ~12 sec · $1–$20 fee · ~15 tx/s L1

Both burn fees permanently (XRP burns all, ETH burns base fee).

XRP supply: 100B fixed, ~57B in circulation. ETH: no hard cap.

elements.content: The math that makes your wallet address provably yours

Your private key is a random 256-bit number. Your public key is derived via elliptic curve multiplication.
Reversing this (finding private from public) is the Elliptic Curve Discrete Log Problem — computationally infeasible.
256-bit private key = 2²⁵⁶ possible values. More than atoms in the observable universe. Brute-force is physically impossible.

actions.text: Public-Key Cryptography
The math that makes your wallet address provably yours
secp256k1: y² = x³ + 7 (mod p)

Your private key is a random 256-bit number. Your public key is derived via elliptic curve multiplication. Reversing this (finding private from public) is the Elliptic Curve Discrete Log Problem — computationally infeasible
256-bit private key = 2²⁵⁶ possible values. More than atoms in the observable universe. Brute-force is physically impossible

elements.content: 700 exahashes per second competing for 3.125 BTC

Difficulty Target

Network sets a target: hash must start with N zeros

Nonce Search

Miners increment a nonce billions of times per second

Block Found

First miner to hit the target broadcasts the block

Adjustment

Every 2016 blocks, difficulty adjusts to maintain 10-min average

actions.text: 700 EH/s. Global hashrate
Bitcoin Mining Economics
700 exahashes per second competing for 3.125 BTC
Difficulty TargetNetwork sets a target: hash must start with N zeros
Nonce SearchMiners increment a nonce billions of times per second
Block FoundFirst miner to hit the target broadcasts the block
RewardBlock subsidy (currently 3.125 BTC) + all tx fees. AdjustmentEvery 2016 blocks, difficulty adjusts to maintain 10-min average

elements.content: On-chain metrics vs technical analysis — what each reveals

📈 On-Chain Analysis

• MVRV: Market cap ÷ Realized cap — above 3.5 = historically overheated

• SOPR: Profit ratio of moved coins — above 1 = profit-taking

• Exchange inflow spikes = sell pressure

• Long-term holder supply = conviction metric

• NVT: Network Value to Transactions ratio

📊 Technical Analysis

• RSI > 70 = overbought, < 30 = oversold

• MACD crossover signals momentum shift

• Bollinger Band squeeze = volatility incoming

• Support/resistance = prior price memory

• Volume confirmation = move has conviction

elements.content: Understanding the rotation and knowing where we are

Altcoin Landscape

Smart contract platforms: ETH, SOL, AVAX
Layer 2s: Arbitrum, Optimism, Base
Privacy: Monero (ring sigs), Zcash (zk-SNARKs)
Payments: XRP, Stellar, Litecoin

Evaluation checklist:
• Who controls the validator set?
• Is the code audited?
• What is the tokenomics / unlock schedule?

Market Cycles

Bitcoin dominance → altseason pattern:
1. BTC pumps (risk-on, store of value)
2. ETH follows (smart contract demand)
3. Altcoins rotate up (speculation)
4. Meme coins peak (euphoria top)

MVRV > 3.5 has historically marked cycle tops. Patience is the edge.

elements.content:
Custody

Who holds your keys? Exchange = their keys. Self-custody = your keys. Not your keys, not your coins.

Exchange Safety

Use regulated exchanges. Enable 2FA. Whitelist withdraw addresses. Don't keep trading stacks on exchange.

DCA Strategy

Dollar-cost averaging removes timing risk. Invest fixed $ weekly regardless of price.

Tax Reality

Every swap, sale, or spend is a taxable event in most jurisdictions. Track cost basis from day one.

actions.text: Owning Crypto Responsibly
Custody, security, strategy, and taxes
CustodyWho holds your keys? Exchange = their keys. Self-custody = your keys. Not your keys, not your coins
Exchange SafetyUse regulated exchanges. Enable 2FA. Whitelist withdraw addresses. Don't keep trading stacks on exchange
DCA StrategyDollar-cost averaging removes timing risk. Invest fixed $ weekly regardless of price
Tax RealityEvery swap, sale, or spend is a taxable event in most jurisdictions. Track cost basis from day one

elements.content: How tokenomics is engineered to create specific behaviors

Token design determines how a token behaves economically. Key variables: total supply, emission schedule, burn mechanisms, vesting schedules for team/investors, and utility requirements.


Deflationary tokens (burn > mint) create scarcity over time. BNB burns a portion of exchange fees. Ethereum burns base fees (EIP-1559). Inflationary tokens fund validators/stakers but dilute holders if emissions exceed demand.

🔒 Vesting
Team tokens locked — reduces dump risk

actions.text: ⚙️ Token Design & Monetary Policy
How tokenomics is engineered to create specific behaviors

Token design determines how a token behaves economically. Key variables: total supply, emission schedule, burn mechanisms, vesting schedules for team/investors, and utility requirements. Deflationary tokens (burn > mint) create scarcity over time. BNB burns a portion of exchange fees. Ethereum burns base fees (EIP-1559). Inflationary tokens fund validators/stakers but dilute holders if emissions exceed demand
📉 Deflationary. Burns exceed mints — scarcity grows
📈 Inflationary. Emissions fund security — dilutes holders
🔒 Vesting. Team tokens locked — reduces dump risk
⚙️ Utility. Burning for use = sustainable tokenomics

elements.content: Crypto that gives you voting power over a protocol

Governance tokens give holders the ability to vote on protocol decisions — fee structures, treasury spending, new features, security parameters. Uniswap's UNI, Compound's COMP, Maker's MKR.


Risks of governance: voter apathy (most holders don't vote), whale domination (large holders control outcomes), and governance attacks (acquiring enough tokens to pass malicious proposals — this happened to Beanstalk Protocol for $182M).

actions.text: 🗳️ Governance Tokens
Crypto that gives you voting power over a protocol

Governance tokens give holders the ability to vote on protocol decisions — fee structures, treasury spending, new features, security parameters. Uniswap's UNI, Compound's COMP, Maker's MKR. Risks of governance: voter apathy (most holders don't vote), whale domination (large holders control outcomes), and governance attacks (acquiring enough tokens to pass malicious proposals — this happened to Beanstalk Protocol for $182M)
🗳️ Vote. Token holders propose and vote
🐳 Whale Risk. Large holders dominate outcomes
😴 Voter Apathy. Low participation = bad decisions
⚔️ Attack. Buy tokens → pass malicious proposal

questions.question: What does UTXO stand for in Bitcoin?
questions.analysis: UTXO = Unspent Transaction Output. Bitcoin tracks coin ownership as a set of unspent outputs rather than account balances.

questions.question: XRP settles transactions in approximately how long?
questions.analysis: XRPL typically settles in 3–5 seconds, compared to Bitcoin's ~10 minute blocks and Ethereum's ~12 second blocks.

questions.question: What happens to XRP fees after each transaction?
questions.analysis: XRPL burns transaction fees permanently — removing them from circulation. This is deflationary and prevents spam.

questions.question: Which TWO metrics are examples of on-chain analysis indicators?
questions.analysis: MVRV and SOPR are on-chain metrics derived from blockchain data. RSI and MACD are traditional technical indicators from price charts.

questions.question: A friend says 'Bitcoin and XRP are basically the same thing — they're both cryptocurrencies.' Which answer best identifies two specific technical differences?

options.label: Bitcoin uses Proof-of-Work mining with ~7 tx/s and 10-minute finality; XRP uses RPCA consensus with 1,500 tx/s and 3–5 second finality
Bitcoin has a fixed supply and XRP does not; both use the same consensus mechanism
XRP is mined by validators while Bitcoin is mined by GPUs; both settle in under a minute
Both use the UTXO model, but XRP burns all fees while Bitcoin does not

questions.analysis: Bitcoin uses Proof-of-Work (miners compete on hash rate), settles in ~10 minutes, and handles ~7 tx/s. XRP uses RPCA consensus (no mining), settles in 3–5 seconds, and handles 1,500 tx/s natively. Both burn fees permanently, but their architecture and consensus are fundamentally different.

questions.question: What is the UTXO model?

options.label: A staking mechanism used by Ethereum validators
Unspent Transaction Outputs — Bitcoin tracks individual coin units not account balances
A token standard for governance on Ethereum
The fee calculation model for XRPL transactions

questions.analysis: Bitcoin doesn't store balances in accounts. It tracks unspent outputs from previous transactions. To spend, you consume UTXOs as inputs and create new outputs. Your 'balance' is the sum of all UTXOs you can unlock with your private key.

questions.question: What is a 'governance attack' and which protocol suffered one for $182M?

options.label: An oracle manipulation — Mango Markets in 2022
Buying enough tokens to pass malicious governance proposals — Beanstalk in 2022
A reentrancy exploit — The DAO in 2016

questions.analysis: Beanstalk's attacker used a flash loan to acquire a supermajority of governance tokens, passed a malicious proposal to drain the treasury, and repaid the flash loan — all in one transaction. $182M was stolen. This exposed the risk of low-quorum, immediate-execution governance.

questions.question: What does 'deflationary tokenomics' mean?

options.label: The token price always decreases over time
The circulating supply decreases over time through burning mechanisms
The token is pegged to a deflationary fiat currency

questions.analysis: Deflationary tokens reduce circulating supply over time. Ethereum burns base fees (EIP-1559) — periods of high demand can make ETH deflationary. BNB burns quarterly based on revenue. Scarcity from burning can support price if demand is stable or growing.

actions.text: Time to test your knowledge! Answer each question carefully — you can review the material after the quiz.
Take your time with these questions. The goal is to solidify your understanding, not just pass the quiz.
